edges.description: AR variants impair receptor expression, ligand binding, DNA binding, or receptor-dependent transcriptional signaling.
Reduced receptor signaling makes androgen-responsive tissues resistant to otherwise age-appropriate androgen exposure.
Complete androgen resistance produces female external genitalia in a 46,XY individual.
Pubic and axillary hair development is reduced because terminal sexual hair growth depends on androgen receptor signaling.
edges.target: Sparse or Absent Terminal Sexual Hair

edges.description: Complete androgen resistance is associated with normal testis development but failure of descent into the scrotum.
Retained undescended gonadal tissue creates a tumor-risk management issue that informs gonadectomy decisions.

evidence.snippet: All of the complete AIS (CAIS) cases were genetically confirmed

evidence.snippet: Sequence analysis revealed that all ten patients harbored AR mutations.

nodes.description: Hemizygous pathogenic variants in AR disrupt the androgen receptor, the X-linked nuclear receptor that mediates testosterone and dihydrotestosterone responses.

evidence.snippet: AR | androgen receptor | hgnc:644 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) in
Pathogenesis is the result of mutations in the X-linked androgen

nodes.description: Dysfunctional androgen receptor protein reduces androgen-dependent nuclear receptor signaling and transcriptional activation in androgen-responsive tissues.

evidence.reference_title: Phenotypic and molecular characteristics of androgen insensitivity syndrome patients.
evidence.snippet: This protein functions as a steroid hormone-activated transcription factor

evidence.reference_title: Novel compound variants of the AR and MAP3K1 genes are related to the clinical heterogeneity of androgen insensitivity syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Impairment in androgen or DNA binding is the most common mechanism

nodes.description: Complete androgen resistance prevents androgen-dependent masculinization of external genitalia and terminal sexual hair despite a 46,XY karyotype and testes.

evidence.snippet: female phenotype in an individual with an XY

evidence.reference_title: Novel compound variants of the AR and MAP3K1 genes are related to the clinical heterogeneity of androgen insensitivity syndrome.
evidence.snippet: a normal amount of testosterone secretion but with symptoms and signs of androgen deficiency or reduction

nodes.description: Affected individuals have female external genitalia in the setting of a 46,XY karyotype and testes, often presenting with inguinal swelling in infancy or primary amenorrhea at puberty.

evidence.snippet: HP:0008730 | Female external genitalia in individual with 46,XY karyotype | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: Phenotypic and molecular characteristics of androgen insensitivity syndrome patients.
evidence.snippet: Affected individuals typically exhibit inguinal swelling during infancy or primary amenorrhea during puberty.

nodes.description: Retained gonadal tissue in CAIS carries recognized tumor risk, including germ cell neoplasia and reported Leydig cell tumors.

evidence.snippet: HP:0100728 | Germ cell neoplasia | Very rare (<4-1%)

evidence.reference_title: Complete androgen insensitivity syndrome: a case report and literature review.

nodes.description: Pubic and axillary hair are sparse or absent in many postpubertal affected individuals because androgen receptor signaling is required for normal androgen-dependent terminal hair development.
nodes.id: Sparse or Absent Terminal Sexual Hair

nodes.description: Testes are commonly intra-abdominal, inguinal, or labial rather than scrotal in complete androgen insensitivity syndrome.
